methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Orbital Inc. is a seed-stage Space & Aerospace company that has raised an undisclosed amount through a16z Speedrun program. The relevant comp universe consists of 5 public companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Standard valuation triangulation methods encounter significant data constraints. Peer-median analysis is inapplicable: while 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, filtering for identical stage, adjacent stage, and similar valuation tier yielded fewer than 3 datapoints, rendering the subset unrepresentative. Public-comp anchoring cannot be executed because Orbital Inc.'s revenue is not on file—a common limitation for private companies at seed stage. The public Space & Aerospace sector trades at a median P/S multiple of 42.9x across the 6 identified tickers, but without disclosed revenue, this multiple cannot be applied to the target.

The absence of applicable quantitative methods reflects the structural illiquidity and information asymmetry typical of early-stage frontier-tech businesses. A credible valuation would require either direct revenue disclosure from Orbital Inc., identification of 3+ seed-stage Space & Aerospace comps with disclosed metrics, or reliance on qualitative assessment of management, technology differentiation, and capital efficiency relative to the top private peer set. Current data does not support a point estimate or range.
